heading: Requirements and restrictions
label: 3241 Requirements and restrictions

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Individuals in on-the-job training or individuals employed in activities under this subchapter shall be compensated at the same rates, including periodic increases, as trainees or employees who are similarly situated in similar occupations by the same employer and who have similar training, experience, and skills, and such rates shall be in accordance with applicable law, but in no event less than the higher of the rate specified in or the applicable State or local minimum wage law.section 206(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: B Rule of construction
children.text: The reference in subparagraph (A) to shall not be applicable for individuals in territorial jurisdictions in which does not apply.section 206(a)(1) of this titlesection 206(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Treatment of allowances, earnings, and payments
children.label: 2 Treatment of allowances, earnings, and payments
children.text: Allowances, earnings, and payments to individuals participating in programs under this subchapter shall not be considered as income for the purposes of determining eligibility for and the amount of income transfer and in-kind aid furnished under any Federal or federally assisted program based on need, other than as provided under the Social Security Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 301

children.heading: Limitations on activities that impact wages of employees
children.label: 1 Limitations on activities that impact wages of employees
children.text: No funds provided under this subchapter shall be used to pay the wages of incumbent employees during their participation in economic development activities provided through a statewide workforce development system.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: A Prohibition
children.text: A participant in a program or activity authorized under this subchapter (referred to in this section as a “specified activity”) shall not displace (including a partial displacement, such as a reduction in the hours of nonovertime work, wages, or employment benefits) any currently employed employee (as of the date of the participation).

children.heading: Prohibition on impairment of contracts
children.label: B Prohibition on impairment of contracts
children.text: A specified activity shall not impair an existing contract for services or collective bargaining agreement, and no such activity that would be inconsistent with the terms of a collective bargaining agreement shall be undertaken without the written concurrence of the labor organization and employer concerned.

children.label: A
children.text: any other individual is on layoff from the same or any substantially equivalent job;

children.label: B
children.text: the employer has terminated the employment of any regular employee or otherwise reduced the workforce of the employer with the intention of filling the vacancy so created with the participant; or

children.label: C
children.text: the job is created in a promotional line that will infringe in any way upon the promotional opportunities of currently employed individuals (as of the date of the participation).

children.heading: Health and safety
children.label: 4 Health and safety
children.text: Health and safety standards established under Federal and State law otherwise applicable to working conditions of employees shall be equally applicable to working conditions of participants engaged in specified activities. To the extent that a State workers’ compensation law applies, workers’ compensation shall be provided to participants on the same basis as the compensation is provided to other individuals in the State in similar employment.

children.heading: Employment conditions
children.label: 5 Employment conditions
children.text: Individuals in on-the-job training or individuals employed in programs and activities under this subchapter shall be provided benefits and working conditions at the same level and to the same extent as other trainees or employees working a similar length of time and doing the same type of work.

children.heading: Opportunity to submit comments
children.label: 6 Opportunity to submit comments
children.text: Interested members of the public, including representatives of businesses and of labor organizations, shall be provided an opportunity to submit comments to the Secretary with respect to programs and activities proposed to be funded under part B.

children.heading: No impact on union organizing
children.label: 7 No impact on union organizing
children.text: Each recipient of funds under this subchapter shall provide to the Secretary assurances that none of such funds will be used to assist, promote, or deter union organizing.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each State and local area receiving an allotment or allocation under this subchapter shall establish and maintain a procedure for grievances or complaints alleging violations of the requirements of this subchapter from participants and other interested or affected parties. Such procedure shall include an opportunity for a hearing and be completed within 60 days after the filing of the grievance or complaint.

children.label: i
children.text: a decision relating to such violation has not been reached within 60 days after the date of the filing of the grievance or complaint and either party appeals to the Secretary; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a decision relating to such violation has been reached within such 60 days and the party to which such decision is adverse appeals such decision to the Secretary.

children.heading: Additional requirement
children.label: B Additional requirement
children.text: The Secretary shall make a final determination relating to an appeal made under subparagraph (A) no later than 120 days after receiving such appeal.

children.label: A
children.text: to suspension or termination of payments under this subchapter;

children.label: B
children.text: to prohibition of placement of a participant with an employer that has violated any requirement under this subchapter;

children.label: C
children.text: where applicable, to reinstatement of an employee, payment of lost wages and benefits, and reestablishment of other relevant terms, conditions, and privileges of employment; and

children.label: D
children.text: where appropriate, to other equitable relief.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 4 Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in paragraph (3) shall be construed to prohibit a grievant or complainant from pursuing a remedy authorized under another Federal, State, or local law for a violation of this subchapter.

children.heading: Prohibition on use of funds to encourage or induce relocation
children.label: 1 Prohibition on use of funds to encourage or induce relocation
children.text: No funds provided under this subchapter shall be used, or proposed for use, to encourage or induce the relocation of a business or part of a business if such relocation would result in a loss of employment for any employee of such business at the original location and such original location is within the United States.

children.heading: Prohibition on use of funds after relocation
children.label: 2 Prohibition on use of funds after relocation
children.text: No funds provided under this subchapter for an employment or training activity shall be used for customized or skill training, on-the-job training, incumbent worker training, transitional employment, or company-specific assessments of job applicants or employees, for any business or part of a business that has relocated, until the date that is 120 days after the date on which such business commences operations at the new location, if the relocation of such business or part of a business results in a loss of employment for any employee of such business at the original location and such original location is within the United States.

children.heading: Repayment
children.label: 3 Repayment
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a violation of paragraph (1) or (2) has occurred, the Secretary shall require the State that has violated such paragraph (or that has provided funding to an entity that has violated such paragraph) to repay to the United States an amount equal to the amount expended in violation of such paragraph.

children.heading: Limitation on use of funds
children.label: e Limitation on use of funds
children.text: No funds available to carry out an activity under this subchapter shall be used for employment generating activities, investment in revolving loan funds, capitalization of businesses, investment in contract bidding resource centers, economic development activities, or similar activities, that are not directly related to training for eligible individuals under this subchapter. No funds received to carry out an activity under part B shall be used for foreign travel.

children.heading: Testing and sanctioning for use of controlled substances
children.label: f Testing and sanctioning for use of controlled substances
A
children.text: testing participants in programs under part B for the use of controlled substances; and

children.label: B
children.text: sanctioning such participants who test positive for the use of such controlled substances.

children.label: i
children.text: with respect to the first occurrence for which a participant tests positive, a State may exclude the participant from the program for a period not to exceed 6 months; and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to the second occurrence and each subsequent occurrence for which a participant tests positive, a State may exclude the participant from the program for a period not to exceed 2 years.

children.heading: Appeal
children.label: B Appeal
children.text: The testing of participants and the imposition of sanctions under this subsection shall be subject to expeditious appeal in accordance with due process procedures established by the State.

children.heading: Privacy
children.label: C Privacy
children.text: A State shall establish procedures for testing participants for the use of controlled substances that ensure a maximum degree of privacy for the participants.

children.heading: Funding requirement
children.label: 3 Funding requirement
children.text: In testing and sanctioning of participants for the use of controlled substances in accordance with this subsection, the only Federal funds that a State may use are the amounts made available for the administration of statewide workforce investment activities under .section 3174(a)(3)(B) of this title

children.heading: Subgrant authority
children.label: g Subgrant authority
children.text: A recipient of grant funds under this subchapter shall have the authority to enter into subgrants in order to carry out the grant, subject to such conditions as the Secretary may establish.
